ocr.text: In the Fiscal Year 2024 report on infrastructure spending allocation, the total investment amounts to KRW 16,500 with a year-
over-year change of 4.2%, holding a market share of 100.0%. The key categories include Transportation at KRW 5,000 with a
5.0% change and 40.0% market share, Water Supply at KRW 1,500 with a 4.0% change and 12.0% market share, Waste
Management at KRW 1,000 with a 2.0% change and 8.0% market share, and Telecommunications at KRW 2,000 with a 6.0%
change and 16.0% market share. Infrastructure investment breakdown shows Energy at KRW 3,000 with a 3.5% change and
24.0% market share, while public facilities account for KRW 1,200, changing by 1.5% with a market share of 9.6%. The
funding sources are Federal Budget at KRW 8,000 with a 5.5% change and 48.5% market share, State Contributions at KRW
5,000 with a 4.0% change, Private Investment at KRW 3,500 with a 3.2% change and 21.2% market share, and Foreign Aid at
KRW 200 with a 0.5% change. The regional allocation includes Northeast at KRW 6,000, Midwest at KRW 4,000, and South at
KRW 6,500. In terms of public pension fund performance, Fund A has total assets of KRW 500B with a return rate of 12.3%
and year-to-date performance of 5.8%, ranking in the top 10%.
